flashcards.front: What does LIFO stand for in context of stacks?
flashcards.back: Last-In-First-Out - the last element added is the first one to be removed

flashcards.front: What are the two primary operations of a stack?

flashcards.back: Condition that occurs when trying to push an element onto a full stack
Condition that occurs when trying to pop an element from an empty stack

flashcards.front: What does the peek operation do?
flashcards.back: Returns the top element without removing it from the stack

flashcards.front: What is the time complexity of basic stack operations?
flashcards.back: O(1) for push, pop, peek, isEmpty, and isFull operations

flashcards.front: What value typically indicates an empty stack in array implementation?
Name two real-world applications of stacks
flashcards.back: Function call management, undo/redo operations, expression evaluation, browser back button

flashcards.front: What is the main limitation of array-based stack implementation?
flashcards.back: Fixed size - cannot dynamically grow beyond the allocated array size

flashcards.front: How is a stack different from a queue?
flashcards.back: Stack follows LIFO principle, while queue follows FIFO (First-In-First-Out) principle
